title: Lord Mirdalan
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Update|[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]}} {{Character |type=Pet |image=[[File:LordMirdalan-SWG5.jpg]] |name=Lord Mirdalan |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Strill]] |gender= |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] |masters= |apprentices= |owners=*[[Walon Vau]] *[[Jaing Skirata]] }} {{Quote|Cute…''"
"''And clever.|[[Sev/Legends|Sev]] sarcasm is lost on Walon Vau|''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''}} '''Lord Mirdalan''', affectionately called '''Mird''' or '''''Mird'ika''''', was a pet [[strill]] belonging to [[Walon Vau]]. Mird was often referred to as "it" in conversation and in writings, which was considered acceptable given strills simultaneously possessed multiple types of [[Reproduction/Legends|reproductive]] organs. Mird was despised by [[Kal Skirata]] and his [[Null-class Advanced Recon Commando|Null troopers]] after the strill attack on a young [[Ordo Skirata|Ordo]] and because of the horrible smell that strills had. At some point in its life on Kamino, Mird killed a lower caste blue-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] during the training of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Mird accompanied Vau to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and accompanied Kal Skirata as part of his civilian disguise in dealing with [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] terrorists, even aiding on a couple of missions along the way. After Walon Vau's bank heist on [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]] of his family's secured holdings, Mirdalan stayed behind with him when he became trapped in an ice worm hole, helping lead Kal Skirata to his location, aid in rescuing him and rewarming him by blanketing Vau's body with its own. Later, he would accompany Vau and Skirata in the ship ''[[Aay'han]]'' on the search for former chief geneticist [[Ko Sai/Legends|Ko Sai]], and then to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], where [[Etain Tur-Mukan]] concealed herself prior to the birth of her son, [[Venku Skirata|Venku]]. The evening before the birth, Mirdalan built a nest and brought a freshly-killed [[shatual]] to honor the occasion, both stunning and touching those present, most notably [[Rav Bralor]]. Lord Mirdalan was very fond of Etain's child, [[Venku Skirata]] and would build nests for the baby, to the disgust of [[Ordo Skirata]], as they were usually made out of his bed sheets and clothes. Lord Mirdalan later came into the possession of [[Jaing Skirata]] when he followed through on his promise to [[Walon Vau]] that he would take care of it when Vau died. When [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and his granddaughter [[Mirta Gev]] were searching for him to reverse the clone degeneration slowly killing Fett, Skirata and Mird ambushed the two in [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. Skirata told them that [[Ko Sai/Legends|Ko Sai's]] data, which they had been seeking, was gone. At the [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] of Mirta and [[Ghes Orade]], Jaing and Mird were present. ==Appearances== *''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' *''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' *''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|Under ''Mird'ika''}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Lord Mirdalan |pl=Lord Mirdalan }} [[Category:Mandalorian culture]] [[Category:Named pets]]
